title: A Non Fiction Book on Evangelion
display_title: A Non Fiction Book on Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: A Non-Fiction Book on Evangelion?             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Apr 03, 2015 8:41 pm Books on the production and influence of animated series aren't unheard of. For example, I own "The Unauthorized Ren & Stimpy Story." It's interesting to me that we don't have such a book for Evangelion, at least not in America. Does such a book exist in Japan? What are the odds of an English-language book?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 03, 2015 10:06 pm Japan does have plenty of "explaining the mysteries of Eva" (FLCL reference) books you find on Amazon JP/etc , though I know nothing about the quality or content. Fans have made their own unauthorized English books on say, Sailor Moon (haven't read it yet), so I guess an English one about Eva is partly a matter of enough fan power behind it.

[]:             Quote Post by Literary Eagle » Sat Apr 04, 2015 10:07 am The staff of Gainax and/or Khara talk about the production of the Evangelion TV series in the books Schizo and Parano , and the production of the 1.0 and 2.0 movies in the Complete Records Collections. But if you're looking for a book that also had an official English release... well, I guess there's The Notenki Memoirs: Studio Gainax and the Men Who Created Evangelion , which was written by one of the founding members of Gainax (and yes, I will be reviewing this book eventually ). It's more about the history of Gainax than the production of Evangelion, though.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Apr 04, 2015 1:50 pm There was one book, ten years ago. It was not very good. http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews/unofficial-guide-to-evangelion.php

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sat Apr 04, 2015 2:42 pm In book form, but not a book per se - there is a master's thesis titled The Other Impact: Neon Genesis Evangelion, Postmodernism and Japan in the 1990s.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Apr 04, 2015 4:40 pm On my shelf. Quite interesting, but not the sort of stuff we talk about here so much.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 04, 2015 6:36 pm ...And then there's the Unofficial Guide to Evangelion. No one should ever read that book. It is equal part useless and inaccurate.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Sun Apr 05, 2015 1:22 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: so I guess an English one about Eva is partly a matter of enough fan power behind it.
